description_long: This object consists of three large, rectangular shipping containers stacked in a staggered arrangement, with two containers on the bottom and one on top. The containers are painted a faded teal color with visible signs of weathering, rust, and dirt, especially around the edges and doors. Each container features reinforced metal corners, vertical ridges along the sides for structural strength, and large double doors on one end, secured with locking bars and handles. The doors display white panels with printed text and codes, as well as yellow warning labels. The sides of the containers are marked with the name 'DON FANG' in blue lettering. The overall appearance suggests these are standard intermodal freight containers commonly used for shipping and storage in industrial or urban environments.
description: A set of three weathered teal shipping containers, stacked with visible doors, locking bars, and industrial markings. The containers show signs of rust, dirt, and printed labels, typical of urban freight storage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double doors and locking bars of the two lower containers, with the top container's side and roof visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, ribbed teal surfaces and 'DON FANG' markings, with the top container offset above the lower two.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, showing the double doors of the lower containers and the top container's side, but from a slightly different angle.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, again showing the ribbed teal surfaces and 'DON FANG' markings, with the top container offset.
attribute_labeling_or_signage: printed logo 'DON FANG' on sides, white panels with codes and yellow warning labels on doors
attribute_handles_or_grips: four vertical locking bars with integrated handles on each door
